channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Judge holds ICE agent in contempt after he detained suspect during a trial
item.description: ICE agent Brian Sullivan detained Wilson Martell-Lebron last week as he was leaving court. But a Boston Municipal Court judge issued a ruling Monday against Sullivan, arguing that he had deprived Martell-Lebron of his rights.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, KREX 5

item.title: April Fool's prank renames French Quarter to American Quarter in New Orleans
item.description: An April Fool’s prank left many New Orleans residents and visitors stunned after a fake website announced that the historic French Quarter had been renamed the “American Quarter” by executive order of Louisiana Gov. Jeff Landry.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Spears, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Detained Tufts student taken from Massachusetts before judge ordered her kept there, government says
item.description: U.S. Justice Department lawyers say a Tufts University doctoral student from Turkey who was detained by immigration authorities had been moved to Vermont by the time a federal judge ordered that she be kept in Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: U.S. soldier from Joliet dies during training exercise in Lithuania
item.description: One of the four U.S. soldiers who died in a training exercise in Lithuania last week was from Joliet.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Barnes, WGN 9

item.title: How to protect (and delete) your DNA data after 23andMe bankruptcy filing
item.description: The genetic testing company 23andMe recently filed for bankruptcy and announced it was looking to sell "substantially all of its assets." Here's what experts say that could mean for Utahns.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: California lawmakers reject bills to ban trans athletes' participation in girls sports
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California lawmakers won't change state policies allowing transgender kids and teens to play on sports teams consistent with their gender identities amid heated nationwide debates over the participation of trans youth in athletics.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dressing sold at Trader Joe's recalled over undeclared allergens
item.description: A California company has issued a voluntary recall for a dressing distributed to 18 states and sold under the Trader Joe's private label.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KTLA 5

item.title: Glendale man among 4 soldiers killed on training mission in Lithuania
item.description: A soldier from Los Angeles County is among the four killed by a training exercise in Lithuania last month. The four soldiers were reported missing last week when their armored vehicle sank in a bog, the New York Times reports. "The four U.S.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: An ‘administrative error’ sent a Maryland man to an El Salvador prison, ICE says
item.description: President Donald Trump’ s administration has acknowledged mistakenly deporting a Maryland man with protected legal status to a notorious El Salvador prison last month, but is arguing against returning him to the United States because of his alleged gang ties and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: University of Minnesota student who was detained by ICE sues for immediate release
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A University of Minnesota graduate business student who's being held by Immigration and Customs Enforcement is suing for his immediate release, saying his arrest violated his rights and he's been given little explanation for why he's being held.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House cancels rest of votes for week after GOP floor rebellion
item.description: House Republican leaders on Tuesday canceled votes for the rest of the week after a band of GOP lawmakers staged a rebellion on the floor, bringing legislative action to a screeching halt. House Majority Whip Tom Emmer (R-Minn.) informed lawmakers the next vote in the House would be Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Northern Michigan
item.description: The tip of Michigan's mitten struggled through another day without electricity Tuesday as restless residents tried to stay warm while utilities scrambled to restore power in a region waylaid by weekend freezing rain that brought down countless trees and poles.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kidney donor detained by ICE before life-saving transplant can take place
item.description: Immigration rights activists are calling on ICE to release a Venezuelan migrant who was preparing to donate a kidney to his brother.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Spinelli, Fox 5

item.title: Social Security Administration probes origin of website portal outage
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security Administration said Tuesday that it is investigating the root cause of website outages that have affected the “my Social Security” portal where recipients access their benefits.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fire kills New York cat sanctuary founder and dozens of animals he rescued
item.description: A fire burned down a Long Island cat shelter, killing its founder, who lived there, and at least 59 of the felines he rescued, authorities said. The fire at the Happy Cat Sanctuary in the hamlet of Medford was reported shortly after 7 a.m. Monday. The cause is under investigation.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal prosecutors to seek death penalty for Luigi Mangione in UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi said Tuesday that she has directed prosecutors to seek the death penalty against Luigi Mangione in the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, following through on the president's campaign promise to vigorously pursue capital punishment.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Yale professor leaving US: Trump launching 'brutal attack' on freedom
item.description: A professor at Yale University said Monday he would be leaving his post and the country over the Trump administration’s “brutal attack” on free speech.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Missouri pastor gets deportation notice for sponsored son from Haiti
item.description: "Do we welcome immigrants in, if they come legally, and are pursing the citizenship process? Is that true or is that not?"
item.dc:creator: Hannah King, KRON4

item.title: Jury selection begins in Karen Read retrial over the death of her Boston police boyfriend
item.description: Read is accused of striking her boyfriend, Boston police officer John O’Keefe, with her SUV and leaving him to die in a snowstorm outside of a house party.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, WFLA, NBC

item.title: TBI cancels Endangered Child Alert out Murfreesboro after 2-year-old found safe
item.description: The Tennessee Bureau of Investigation (TBI) issued an Endangered Child Alert for a missing 2-year-old girl out of Murfreesboro, who has since been found safe.
item.dc:creator: Aubriella Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Democrat will block Trump’s VA nominees to protest cuts
item.description: Sen. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) says he will put a hold on President Trump’s nominees to lead the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to protest cuts at the agency that provides lifelong health care services to military veterans.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: House Intel Democrats call for damage assessment on Signal chat
item.description: Democrats on the House Intelligence Committee on Monday requested an independent assessment into Trump administration officials' use of Signal to discuss an airstrike, asking that Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard initiate such a process and delegate it to another official.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Cast for Beatles biopics revealed: Paul Mescal, Barry Keoghan, Joseph Quinn and Harris Dickinson to star
item.description: Sony Pictures kicked off the annual CinemaCon convention and trade show in Las Vegas on Monday by touting their four upcoming biopics told from the perspective of each member of The Beatles.
item.dc:creator: Krysta Fauria, Fox 5

item.title: Aggressive sea lion attacks 15-year-old girl in Southern California waters
item.description: A 15-year-old in Long Beach, California, is recovering after a terrifying and unexpected ocean encounter with a sea lion that attacked her over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Musk says he sent MAGA influencer Ashley St. Clair $2.5 million, open to paternity test
item.description: Musk’s comments came after St. Clair was seen selling her Tesla, saying she was doing so after Musk allegedly cut childcare payments.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Girl, 9, dies after procedure at California dental office
item.description: A report from the San Diego County Medical Examiner's Office claims the girl was discharged from the facility and remained asleep on the ride home.
item.dc:creator: Delaney White, WGN 9

item.title: Evacuations ordered as winds pose challenges for firefighters battling California blaze
item.description: BISHOP, Calif. (AP) — Firefighters stopped the forward spread of a wildfire tearing through a remote area of eastern California, but evacuation orders for residents of hundreds of homes remained in place, officials said Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge postpones Trump Administration effort to rescind deportation protections for Venezuelans
item.description: On Monday, a federal judge in California issued a temporary injunction, postponing a Trump Administration effort to rescind deportation protections.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Rogers, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A court is allowing the release of most records in the Gene Hackman death investigation
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A court on Monday cleared the way for the release of investigative records from the deaths of Gene Hackman and his wife, Betsy Arakawa, as long as depictions of the deceased couple are blocked from view.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Explosion at Detroit apartment building injures residents, including several children
item.description: When firefighters arrived, some people were in windows of the building “kind of hanging off ready to jump,” Fire Commissioner Chuck Simms told reporters.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Photos show living conditions of Connecticut man allegedly held captive for over 20 years
item.description: Dozens of photos obtained by Nexstar's WTNH show the living conditions authorities found in the Waterbury, Connecticut, home where a man says he was held captive by his stepmom, Kimberly Sullivan, for over 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘not joking’ about a third term. Republicans insist he is
item.description: Congressional Republicans are brushing off President Trump’s latest comments about seeking a third term, downplaying them as a joke designed to needle the media even as the president insists he is serious.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Report: 1 in 5 Catholics in US affected by deportations
item.description: One in five Catholic families in the United States stands to feel the pain from the Trump administration's promised mass deportations, a group of faith leaders said.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: NASA astronauts Butch Wilmore, Suni Williams discuss extended ISS stay
item.description: NASA astronauts Butch Wilmore blasted into orbit in June 2024, as part of a test flight of Boeing’s new Starliner crew capsule. They were scheduled to return about eight days later, but problems plagued the mission.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Cause of death determined for 3 young American women who died at Belize resort
item.description: A cause of death has been determined for three young women from the United States found dead in their hotel room at a resort in San Pedro, Belize, late last month, NBC News reported. The trio were discovered deceased on Feb.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: Duke's great D sends Blue Devils to their 18th Final Four with an 85-65 win over Alabama
item.description: Duke freshman Cooper Flagg swooped across the paint and swatted an Alabama player’s shot completely off the court. The ball landed in the stands. Flagg and the Blue Devils -- they’re headed to the Final Four.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: At least 4 dead after severe storms in the South Texas-Mexico border region and hundreds rescued
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Drenching rains along the Texas-Mexico border trapped hundreds of people in flooded homes and in cars stranded in high waters, scrambling rescue crews to calls for help that continued Friday even as the downpours let up. At least four people died, including some who drowned.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ and JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4
